context.story_engine: A high school teacher builds a drug empire, testing how far he'll go for family and control

plotlines.name: Walt: Empire
plotlines.goal: establish himself as Gus's lead chemist and secure his position in the superlab operation
plotlines.obstacle: Gus's manipulation and control, the threat of being replaced by Gale, cartel violence
plotlines.stakes: death, loss of financial security, family exposure

plotlines.name: Walt: Family
plotlines.goal: maintain control over his family despite Skyler's knowledge of his crimes
plotlines.obstacle: Skyler's affair with Ted, divorce proceedings, restraining order
plotlines.stakes: complete family breakdown, loss of children, isolation

plotlines.name: Jesse: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive and profit in the drug trade while navigating his partnership with Walt
plotlines.obstacle: homelessness, inferior product quality, violent dealers, Gus's suspicion

plotlines.name: Hank: Investigation
plotlines.goal: track down the blue meth operation and its creators
plotlines.obstacle: panic attacks, career suspension, physical injuries from cartel attack

plotlines.name: Jesse: Love
plotlines.goal: build a genuine relationship with Andrea and protect her family
plotlines.obstacle: his criminal life, Andrea's brother's involvement with dealers, his own deception
plotlines.stakes: loss of love, Andrea's safety, moral corruption

plotlines.name: Gus: Control
plotlines.goal: maintain absolute control over his drug operation and eliminate threats
plotlines.obstacle: Walt's unpredictability, cartel pressure, DEA investigation

plotlines.name: Salamanca Twins: Vengeance
plotlines.goal: kill Walter White to avenge Tuco's death
plotlines.obstacle: Gus's interference and redirection toward Hank

events.event: Leonel and Marco Salamanca pin Walt's sketch to a shrine and head north from Mexico
People crawl on their bellies toward the shrine in the Mexican village
Walt gives a tone-deaf statistical speech at the school assembly about the plane crash
Skyler demands a divorce from Walt after discovering his meth cooking
Walt refuses to move out and insists he did everything for the family
Jesse is released from rehab and discovers his parents sold his aunt's house
Jesse checks into a motel with diminished cash and calls Walt who doesn't answer
Hank receives a promotion to DEA's El Paso field office near the cartel border
Hank publicly celebrates but privately shows anxiety from his Tuco encounter
Walt throws pizza on the roof when Skyler locks him out, then enters through back door
Skyler takes the children and leaves for the night
Gus monitors the situation from a distance through his network
The Salamanca twins cross the border and execute immigrants who recognize them
Walt sits alone in the empty house, convinced he can hold his family together

episodes.theme: the illusion of control and consequences

interactions.description: all storylines explore how characters attempt to control situations that are spiraling beyond their grasp
Walt believes he can control his family situation while deadly assassins hunt him
the twins' hunt for Walt will intersect with his drug operation

events.event: The Salamanca twins enter Walt's house with an axe and wait on his bed while he showers
Gus calls the twins away before they can kill Walt
Walt continues his morning routine, oblivious to the near-death experience
Skyler hires a divorce attorney
Walt refuses to sign divorce papers and tells Walt Jr. that Skyler is breaking up the family
Walt Jr. turns against Skyler after Walt's manipulation
Skyler reveals she knows everything about Walt's meth operation and threatens to tell Hank
Walt calls Skyler's bluff, betting she won't destroy the family's reputation
Jesse, broke and homeless, gets help from Saul to find a new duplex
Jesse visits his old neighborhood and sees strangers in his aunt's former house
Hank suffers a panic attack in the DEA elevator before his El Paso transfer
Hank forces himself back to composure and tells no one about the panic attack
The Salamanca twins visit their paralyzed uncle Hector in the nursing home
Hector's rage confirms the twins' mission to kill Walt for Tuco's death
Gus meets with the twins through his intermediary and counsels patience
Walt attempts a family dinner with takeout, acting as if nothing has changed
Skyler returns home disgusted but sits down powerless to explain the truth
Walt smiles, believing he is winning the standoff through attrition

interactions.description: all storylines explore different forms of control — Walt's manipulation of his family, Gus's orchestration of violence, and the twins' single-minded pursuit of revenge
Walt is oblivious to how close he came to death while the audience witnessed the twins' near-attack
Skyler threatens to tell Hank the truth while the audience knows Hank is psychologically unraveling
Gus directly intervenes in the twins' mission, showing his power over their revenge plot

events.event: Walt sleeps in the master bedroom while Skyler takes the couch, each refusing to yield ground
Skyler meets with her divorce attorney who explains Walt will likely get shared custody without admissible evidence
Skyler begins an affair with Ted Beneke at Beneke Fabricators
Skyler tells Walt 'I fucked Ted' when he comes home
Jesse tries to cook meth alone using Walt's formula but produces inferior product
Jesse sells low-quality meth through Skinny Pete, drawing complaints
Saul approaches Walt with Gus's offer of three million for three months in the superlab
Walt declines Gus's offer, insisting he is done with the drug business
Hank investigates a tip connecting the blue meth to local market, noting lower quality suggests copycat
The Salamanca twins acquire chrome axes, preparing to strike
Gus meets with the twins and persuades them to wait, promising to deliver Tuco's killer on his timeline
Walt drives recklessly after Skyler's confession, nearly causing an accident, then sits gripping the steering wheel

interactions.description: all storylines explore control slipping away — Skyler takes control from Walt, Walt refuses control of the empire, Gus controls the twins' vengeance
Hank investigates the blue meth without knowing Walt created the formula Jesse is copying
Gus directly intervenes to control the twins' timeline for killing Walt

events.event: Walt confronts Ted Beneke at his office and makes a veiled threat
Security escorts Walt out of Ted's office
Walt smashes a glass table in frustration at home
Walt Jr. blames Skyler for provoking his father
Skyler files a restraining order against Walt
Police arrive to remove Walt from the house
Walt moves into a small condo apartment
Jesse sells his substandard meth and grows frustrated with the quality
Jesse visits Saul asking for new distribution contacts
Saul tells Jesse that Gus wants Walt and there's a place for Jesse if he brings Walt back
Jesse dismisses the idea, insisting he can build his own business
Hank picks a bar fight with two strangers to feel a rush of control
A bouncer pulls Hank off the strangers
Marie notices Hank's behavioral changes and presses him
Hank deflects Marie's concerns with jokes
Gus invites Walt to tour the superlab
Walt sees the state-of-the-art equipment worth millions
Gus appeals to Walt's pride, saying he should not hide from his gifts
Walt declines Gus's offer again but is visibly tempted
Gus accepts the refusal calmly but tells Walt the offer stands
The Salamanca twins surveil Walt from a distance
Gus's associate redirects the twins' vengeance toward Hank instead of Walt
The twins accept Hank as their new target
Walt sits alone in his bare apartment, staring at the three-million-dollar napkin

episodes.theme: the cost of pride and isolation

interactions.description: all storylines explore characters isolated by their pride and choices — Walt alone in his apartment, Jesse insisting on independence, Hank hiding his trauma
the audience knows the twins now target Hank, but Hank remains unaware of the threat
Gus manipulates the twins' vengeance to serve his control, redirecting them from Walt to Hank

events.event: Gus and Max pitch their blue meth to Don Eladio at his poolside estate
Don Eladio orders Hector to execute Max as punishment for cooking without permission
Gus is forced to watch his partner die beside the pool
Don Eladio spares Gus because of unexplained connections in Chile
Gus invites Walt to dinner at his modest home and cooks a meal himself
Gus shares his philosophy that a man provides for his family even when not appreciated
Walt accepts Gus's offer of three months cooking for $15 million total
Walt calls Jesse to inform him of the deal, but Jesse is angry and feels betrayed
Walt visits the superlab for the first time as lead chemist and meets Gale
Gale admires Walt's work, calling the blue meth the purest he has ever analyzed
Walt and Gale cook together and achieve 99.1 percent purity
Hank discovers the blue meth has returned at original purity, meaning the original cook is active
Jesse cooks his own batch in his duplex and begins selling through his crew

interactions.description: all three storylines explore different aspects of providing and sacrifice — Walt providing for family through crime, Gus's decades-long quest for vengeance as providing justice for Max, Jesse providing for himself by going independent
Hank realizes the original cook is back just as Walt officially becomes Gus's lead chemist
Walt's acceptance of Gus's offer directly creates Jesse's independent operation, setting up territorial conflict

events.event: Walt and Gale work together in the superlab, producing large quantities of blue meth with consistent purity
Walt notices Gale is logging every step of the process in a personal notebook, learning to replicate the cook independently
Walt realizes that once Gale masters the formula, Gus will no longer need Walt and could dispose of him
Walt manufactures a complaint about Gale's performance, telling Gus that Gale is not up to standard and demands Jesse as his assistant
Gus, skeptical of Jesse's reliability, reluctantly agrees and reassigns Gale to another facility
Jesse accepts the offer and joins Walt in the superlab
Hank's investigation leads him to the RV through records checks, tracing it to Jesse Pinkman
Hank confronts Jesse at his duplex about the RV
Jesse calls Walt in a panic about Hank finding the RV
Walt races to the junkyard where the RV is stored with Old Joe
Hank follows Jesse's phone GPS to the junkyard and arrives as Walt hides inside the RV
Old Joe blocks Hank from entering the RV by citing it as a domicile, requiring a warrant
Walt has Saul's secretary call Hank with a fake emergency about Marie being in a car accident
Hank abandons the warrant and races to the hospital, giving Old Joe time to crush the RV
Old Joe crushes the RV into a cube of scrap metal
Hank arrives at the hospital, finds Marie perfectly fine, and realizes he has been tricked
Enraged Hank drives to Jesse's duplex, pounds on the door, and beats Jesse severely when he opens it
Jesse calls 911 after being beaten by Hank

interactions.description: all three storylines explore survival tactics — Walt manipulates to survive Gus's control, Jesse survives by partnering with Walt, Hank's investigation threatens everyone's survival
the audience knows Walt is hiding in the RV while Hank searches for evidence just feet away
Walt's manipulation to get Jesse as his partner serves both his empire goals and survival needs

events.event: Flashback shows young Hank receiving his first major bust commendation and calling Marie to celebrate
Jesse lies in hospital with shattered face and tells his attorney he will press full charges against Hank
Hank is suspended from DEA and forced to surrender gun and badge
Marie pleads with Jesse through Saul to drop charges but Jesse refuses
Walt visits Jesse and persuades him to settle for 50-50 partnership in superlab cook
Jesse agrees to drop charges in exchange for equal partnership with Walt
Hank sinks into depression at home without work
ASAC Merkert warns Hank the assault charge could end his career permanently
Marie tries to support Hank but he pushes her away
Gus recognizes disgraced DEA agent is less dangerous and does nothing to intervene
Salamanca twins acquire chrome-plated fire axes and plan attack on Hank
Gus anonymously tips off Hank with one-minute warning about the twins
Twins arrive and open fire on unarmed Hank in parking lot
Hank uses SUV to ram Leonel, crushing his legs
Marco buries axe in Hank's back
Hank finds Leonel's gun, loads hollow-point bullet, and shoots Marco through the head
Paramedics arrive to find Hank collapsed beside two cartel assassins

interactions.description: all storylines explore how violence begets violence and the price of survival in a dangerous world
Gus orchestrates events while Hank remains unaware of who is manipulating his fate
the twins' quest for revenge directly collides with Hank's survival, creating the episode's climactic showdown

events.event: Walt, Jesse, Skyler, Walt Jr., and Marie rush to the hospital after learning Hank has been shot
Hank undergoes surgery for multiple gunshot wounds and severe spinal injury
Marie alternates between grief and rage, demanding answers from the DEA about the cartel hitmen
ASAC Merkert and the DEA team flood the hospital, launching a full investigation
Walt realizes the twins were hunting him and someone redirected them to Hank
Walt confronts Gus in the hospital parking lot about orchestrating the scenario
Gus confirms he pointed the twins at Hank, then tipped Hank off to neutralize the cartel threat
Walt realizes he is deeply in Gus's debt despite being horrified at using Hank as bait
Jesse cooks a batch alone at the superlab, achieving 96 percent purity
Jesse realizes he is more capable than he believed
Leonel Salamanca survives surgery but loses both legs
DEA agents guard Leonel's room, hoping he will identify who sent him
Gus visits Leonel posing as a Los Pollos Hermanos community representative
Mike injects Leonel with a lethal substance when guards step away, killing him
Hank wakes from surgery and learns he may never walk again
Marie holds Hank's hand as he stares at the ceiling
Walt returns home alone, understanding the full scope of Gus's ruthlessness

episodes.theme: the price of power and protection

interactions.description: all storylines explore how protection comes at a devastating cost — Gus protects Walt by sacrificing Hank, Walt's family pays for his empire, Hank's investigation costs him his mobility
the family grieves for Hank not knowing Walt's crimes caused the attack
Gus's manipulation eliminates the Salamanca threat while binding Walt deeper into his operation

events.event: Hank lies conscious but paralyzed in his hospital bed, beginning physical rehabilitation
Marie pushes doctors for aggressive treatment and learns about expensive Colorado spinal therapy facility
Marie brings the financial problem to Skyler about Hank's treatment costs
Skyler proposes using Walt's drug money for Hank's treatment with gambling cover story
Walt agrees to fund Hank's recovery to keep family obligated and explain future cash
Skyler crafts detailed gambling narrative and rehearses Walt on specifics
Jesse and Walt cook together in the superlab but Jesse grows restless with routine
Jesse skims meth from each cook and hides it in his locker before weighing
Jesse sells stolen meth through Badger and Skinny Pete for independent revenue
Gus's distribution manager notices blue meth appearing outside controlled channels
Distribution manager reports unauthorized blue meth sales to Gus
Jesse meets Andrea Cantillo at Narcotics Anonymous and begins spending time with her
Jesse learns Andrea has a young son named Brock and develops genuine feelings
Andrea reveals her brother Tomas works as corner boy for dealers who killed Combo
Jesse confronts reality that his drug profits destroy children in his community

interactions.description: all storylines explore how characters use others to justify their choices — Walt uses family obligation, Jesse uses profit motive, but both face the human cost
Skyler and Walt use drug money to help the DEA agent hunting them
Jesse's independent operation threatens Gus's control, setting up future conflict

events.event: Walt becomes obsessed with a fly in the superlab and refuses to cook until it's eliminated
Jesse considers Walt's fly concern absurd but goes along with the hunt
They spend hours chasing the fly with makeshift tools and equipment
Jesse climbs equipment and nearly falls while hunting the fly
Walt, exhausted and sleep-deprived, begins talking about the night Jane died
Walt tells Jesse there's a moment when a person has gone too far
Jesse listens but doesn't understand the full weight of Walt's words
Jesse slips a sleeping pill into Walt's coffee to end the obsession
Walt drowses on the lab floor while Jesse kills the fly
Jesse shows Walt the dead fly and they resume cooking
The batch is completed and meets purity standards
Walt notices a discrepancy in the yield and suspects theft
Walt watches Jesse with new suspicion but says nothing
Jesse drives home with stolen meth in his glove compartment
Walt lies in bed staring at the ceiling where another fly lands

interactions.description: both storylines explore contamination — Walt's obsession with the fly mirrors his guilt contaminating everything, while Jesse's theft contaminates their partnership
the audience knows Walt let Jane die and that Jesse is stealing, while each character is unaware of what the other has done
the fly serves as a metaphor for Walt's guilt and the contamination of his moral boundaries

events.event: Jesse attends Narcotics Anonymous meetings to identify potential customers for stolen meth
Jesse meets Andrea Cantillo at NA and initially targets her as a buyer
Jesse spends time with Andrea and her son Brock, developing genuine feelings
Andrea reveals her brother Tomas has been recruited as a corner boy by local dealers
Jesse realizes Tomas is the child who killed Combo on the dealers' orders
Walt reviews lab logs and calculates someone has been skimming product
Walt confronts Jesse about stealing meth to sell through Skinny Pete and Badger
Jesse admits to the theft and dismisses Walt's warnings about Gus
Gus and Mike are already aware of the missing product but haven't acted
Skyler coaches Walt on gambling cover story details for his drug money
Skyler presents gambling story to Hank and Marie to justify paying for therapy
Hank accepts the gambling explanation without scrutiny due to his depression
Jesse sees Tomas outside Andrea's apartment, confronting the reality of the child's situation
Jesse sits in his car with his gun, contemplating action against the dealers

interactions.description: all three storylines explore how criminal activity destroys innocent relationships and family bonds
Gus knows about Jesse's theft while Walt and Jesse remain unaware of being watched
Hank unknowingly accepts money from the criminal he's hunting to pay for his own recovery
Jesse's romantic feelings for Andrea collide with the violent reality of his criminal world through Tomas

events.event: Mike tells Walt the story of the domestic violence call and the half measures
Jesse buys a pistol and tells Walt he's going to kill the dealers
Walt pleads with Jesse to stand down and goes to Gus for a meeting
Gus arranges a desert meeting with the dealers, Walt, Jesse, and Mike
Walt demands justice for Tomas but Gus sides with his dealers
Jesse agrees to the truce under duress
Jesse drives to the dealers' corner with his gun drawn
Walt's car slams into both dealers at full speed
Walt shoots the surviving dealer in the head at point-blank range
Walt tells Jesse to run and Jesse flees the scene
Gus receives word of the killings and realizes Walt is now an enemy
Mike drives to the scene to assess the damage

interactions.description: all three storylines explore the consequences of choosing loyalty over pragmatism — Walt chooses Jesse over Gus, Jesse chooses vengeance for Tomas over survival, Gus must choose between his dealers and his operation
all storylines physically converge in the street confrontation where Walt's choice to save Jesse destroys his relationship with Gus
the audience understands that Walt's heroic gesture has actually doomed his position with Gus, while Walt believes he's solved the problem

events.event: Flashback shows Walt and Skyler touring their future house when Walt was still a Gray Matter partner
Gus reinstates Gale in the superlab to learn Walt's cooking process
Walt recognizes that Gale's presence means his death sentence
Walt confronts Gus directly, arguing he and Jesse are indispensable
Mike begins following Walt and Jesse on Gus's orders
Walt meets with Saul to discuss escape options via disappearer
Walt calculates the cost against family obligations and rejects escape
Walt concludes Gale must die to ensure his survival
Walt approaches Jesse with the plan to kill Gale
Jesse recoils at killing innocent Gale, Walt argues it's them or Gale
Mike picks up Walt at gunpoint and drives him to the superlab
Gus decides to kill Walt tonight and have Gale take over the lab
Walt pleads for one phone call to say goodbye to his family
Walt calls Jesse and gives him Gale's address in coded language
Jesse drives across Albuquerque to Gale's apartment
Gale opens the door, recognizes something is wrong, and begs Jesse not to do this
Jesse, crying, raises his gun and pulls the trigger

interactions.description: all storylines explore how far characters will go to protect what they value, corrupting their moral foundations
Mike and Victor believe Walt is calling his family, but the audience knows he's orchestrating Gale's murder
all three storylines converge in the final moments as Jesse's action simultaneously saves Walt, destroys Gus's plan, and forces Jesse to cross a moral line
the flashback serves as emotional counterpoint, showing Walt's lost innocence and potential
